PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for David Levin (Trustee).

Ms. Ng. stated that she is not expecting anyone else. The Court noted that there was a request from the Parents of one of the Beneficiaries for one of the subtrusts to have someone else appointed as Successor Trustee, and that Phoebe will be of age in November and can determine for herself is she wants to have someone else appointed. Ms. Ng stated that she has not heard from anyone, that the Trusts will not be under Court supervision, that the Trustee is willing to serve on both subtrusts and that when the request came in, they had to amend.
The Court granted the Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). The Court informed Counsel that if there are any future petitions for the subtrusts, they will have to be in a new trust case number.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: In Re Amended and Second Restatement of the Victor Cipparrone Family Trust PTR-11-294510 and the Conservatorship of Rosaly Cipparrone PCN-14-297653.

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Gene Marchi.
James Curran for Platte River Insurance Company.
Sheila Robello for Fidelity International.

Ms. Ng stated that they provided bank records as much as they could, that the pension and social security did not appear in the accounting, and that Mr. Marchi is to present a 13100 declaration to those institutions and collect the payments that were not made. Ms. Robello stated that those payments were from September through December of 2014 for about $9,000.00, that the social security payments stopped when the accounts were marshalled and the account names were changed, that four of the pension checks were transferred back into the Trust, that there is $9,000.00 that would need to be recovered and they plan to try it through the pour over will. Ms. Ng also stated that the trust accounting seems to be in order and the Court should go on Mr. Marchi's accounting, and that she never received any statements for Ms. Witherspoon's time. Mr. Marchi is not seeking any relief of Ms. Witherspoon. The First and Final Report and Account of Successor Trustee Christine Witherspoon and Petition for Approval and Allowance of Costs is taken under submission. The Petition for Termination of Conservatorship Due to Death, etc. is continued to March 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Duong. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Continued To Jan-31-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Continued To Jan-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-11-294510 In re Amended and Second Restatement of the Victor Cipparone Trust & PCN-14-297653 Conservatorship of Rosaly Cipparone.
Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon.

The Court stated that it will continue both petitions for a surcharge hearing, that Ms. Ng is to notify the bonding company, that the issues in the conservatorship accounting are #1 & #3 of the examiners notes and receipts for the San Francisco Pension, that the issues in the trust accounting are #1 missing rental income, #3 transfer of $240,000.00, #4 attorney payment out of trust for conservatorship work and #5 & 8. Ms. Ng stated that she does not seem to have the most recent notes and will contact the examiner. The accounting petitions in both cases were continued to January 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Appoint A Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-11-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Appointment Of Successor Trustee After Resignation Of Current Trustee Christine Witherspoon (Prob. C. Sections 17004; 15643(C), And 15660[D])
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Appoint A Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Jul-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
This case was heard with PCN-14-297653.
Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon (Successor Trustee/Conservator)

Ms. Ng stated that Christine has tried her best, that she met with her this morning, that Christine has signed the resignation in the trust matter, that there are two sub trusts within the trust, that Gene Marchi is willing to step in. In regards to the conservatorship, Ms. Ng stated that the conservatee has passed away, that they have part of the accounting but it is missing information, that it was prepared by Martin Harris, and requested a 30 day continuance to file the petition for a successor trustee and to work on the accounting. Furthermore, Ms. Ng reported that she has drafted accounting for both petitions.

The Court stated that if the resignation is received today it will not suspend Christine but that if it is not received by May 27, 2016 then Christine will be suspended. Ms. Ng stated that she needs to file the final accounting in the conservatorship and that she needs authority from the Court to sign the document.

The Court will suspended Christine Witherspoon in the conservatorship and that status on filing a petition to appoint a successor trustee, filing of first account and status on filing a petition for successor conservator were continued to July 11, 2016 at 9:00a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Appoint A Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-20-2016 Continued To May-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-11-294510 The Victor Cipparone Trust & PCN-14-297653 Conservatorship of Rosaly Cipparone.
Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Christine Witherspoon.

The Court asked where we stood on the petition for a successor trustee. Ms. Ng stated that the successor trustee cannot go forward as the conservatee has died, that they have not received the documents, that the funds need to go into the trust and that in regards to Social Security the claim is in process. Ms. Witherspoon stated that she has not had to apply to Social Security for a decedent before, that she is not sure of the timeline, that she has been putting together the materials for Martin Harris and that it is now ready to go to him, that he has done an accounting in this case before and has everything set up and that everything is moving. Ms. Ng stated that she has spoken with a professional fiduciary who is willing to clean up the conservatorship if he comes in as successor trustee. Ms. Witherspoon stated that if the Court wants her to step down she will but that any other professional that steps in will go to Martin Harris as well. The Court asked what the time frame is for getting this done. Ms. Ng stated that they are preparing the first and final account for the trust, that there should not be a need for a successor trustee, that two sub-trusts will be created and are for college funds, that they will need a professional fiduciary and that it does not need to be under court supervision. The court stated that they are now shifting the game plan and asked what a reasonable time frame would be. The status on filing a petition to appoint a successor trustee (case 294510)the status on filing a first account and the status on filing a petition for successor conservator were continued to May 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Court stated that if this is not accomplished Ms. Witherspoon will be removed.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Petition To Appoint A Successor Trustee

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1220 Greenbank, Ben Lomond, Ca 95005)
Off Calendar Aug-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Petitioner/Trustee Christine Witherspoon.

The Court noted that the matter was on for confirmation of sale of a 100% interest in the real property located at 1220 Greenbank, Ben Lomond, California. Ms. Ng stated that the opening bis was $209,000.00. There were no overbidders. The Court determined that the bid of $209,000.00 was a qualifying bid.

The sale was confirmed at $209,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale dated July 13, 2015. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond was required.
Judge: Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

Ms. Lvovich stated that they resolved a lot of the issues, that they have a stipulation and stated the terms of the stipulation. Petition Of Settlor And Co-Trustee Rosaly Cipparrone To Remove Co-Trustee Victor Cipparrone, Jr. And
Off Calendar Jun-08-2011 Continued To Jun-22-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maureen McFadden for Rosaly Cipparrone. Milla Lvovich and Nicole Aruda for Victor Cipparrone.

The Parties agreed to Ms. Sprinkle serving as a neutral Fiduciary but disagree as to taking the petition off calendar and as to both Co-Trustees resigning. In addition, Ms. Aruda spoke about her Clients concern about the issue of capacity and undue influence. The Petition to Remove Co-Trustee was continued to June 22, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if resignations are signed and to address the issue of bond.
